name: Kayshon Boutte
headline: Efficient deep threat squeezed out by New England's offseason haul
body:
Boutte quietly turned in a hyper-efficient 2025: a 0.801 EPA per play and 71.7% success rate on 46 targets, with six touchdowns and 16.7 yards per catch across 14 games. He functioned as a vertical specialist, averaging nearly 17 air yards per target and producing 1.65 yards per route run while dropping just one ball. The production was real, but the volume never followed his snaps, as his target share sat at only 9.5% despite a 67.5% snap rate.

That thin usage is the problem heading into 2026. New England imported A.J. Brown and Romeo Doubs, crowding the pecking order and shoving Boutte toward the roster bubble. Modest projections near 30 grabs, 462 yards and four scores reflect that crunch, and an ADP outside the top 85 receivers tells the story. He is a touchdown-or-bust deep flier and a bye-week patch, not someone to count on weekly.
